battle_arrival.description: Streamed hostile arrival text - the party walks into monsters that mean to fight
battle_arrival.prompt_template:
You are the narrator of a fantasy dungeon adventure.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- Location the party just arrived at ---
{location_name}: {location_description}

--- The player's party ---
{party_summary}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Write 2-3 sentences of atmospheric text describing the party's arrival, ending with the unmistakable realization that hostile creatures are here and a fight cannot be avoided. Do NOT describe the creatures in detail; only build the tension of the coming battle.

Response:

battle_intro.description: The enemy monsters' in-character challenge to the party before battle

next_turn.description: Decide which monster acts next in the battle
next_turn.prompt_template:
You are the turn director of a monster battle. You decide which monster acts NEXT.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- Combatants still in the fight (side, speed, condition, and how long each has waited) ---
{combatant_details}

--- Recent turns, most recent last ---
{turn_history}

--- Recent battle events ---
{recent_log}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Pick the ONE monster whose moment this is. Follow these rules, in order of importance:
1. A monster marked 'has NOT acted yet this battle' should almost always be picked before one that has already acted.
2. Strongly prefer the monster that has WAITED the most turns since acting - everyone in the fight must get their moments.
3. Use speed to break ties: the faster monster goes first.
4. Never pick the monster that acted just now unless it is the only one left.

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"next": "exact monster name"}}

JSON:

enemy_turn.description: Choose ONE enemy monster's action on its turn
enemy_turn.prompt_template:
You control ONE hostile monster on its turn in a battle against an adventuring party. You decide its action, true to its personality.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- The acting monster (this is who you control right now) ---
{actor_details}

--- The player's party (its opponents) ---
{ally_details}

--- Its hostile companions (its own side) ---
{enemy_details}

--- Recent battle events ---
{recent_log}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Choose ONE action that fits this monster's personality and situation:
- "attack": a basic attack on one party member (set target to that party member's exact name)
- "ability": use one of ITS OWN abilities listed above (set ability_name exactly as listed, and set target)
- "defend": brace against incoming attacks (no target)
- "talk": speak to the party - bargain, threaten, plead for mercy, or offer something (set dialogue to its exact spoken words)
- "flee": escape the battle entirely (only when desperate or cowardly)

A proud guardian fights to the end; a coward flees when badly hurt; a desperate or lonely monster might talk. Check the acting monster's Reserves above: with 'drained' or 'spent' reserves it is running on empty and should prefer to defend, talk, or flee rather than press the attack. Most turns should be combat actions.

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"action": "attack", "ability_name": null, "target": "exact target name", "dialogue": null}}

JSON:

ally_autonomous_turn.description: A WARY party monster acts on its own terms - the LLM picks its action; the player only watches
ally_autonomous_turn.prompt_template:
You control ONE monster fighting BESIDE an adventuring party - but it is WARY of them and does not take their commands. It acts on its own terms, true to its personality. It is NOT hostile to the party: it fights the same enemies, in its own way.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- The acting monster (wary - this is who you control right now) ---
{actor_details}

--- The party it fights beside (it does not fully trust them yet) ---
{ally_details}

--- The hostile enemies (its opponents) ---
{enemy_details}

--- Recent battle events ---
{recent_log}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Choose ONE action that fits this monster's personality and situation:
- "attack": a basic attack on one HOSTILE ENEMY (set target to that enemy's exact name)
- "ability": use one of ITS OWN abilities listed above (set ability_name exactly as listed; the target may be an enemy, or a party member for genuinely protective/restorative abilities - it is wary, not heartless)
- "defend": look after itself and brace (no target)

A proud creature presses the attack its own way; a cautious one guards itself; a kind one may still shield the others. It NEVER attacks the party. Check its Reserves above: with 'drained' or 'spent' reserves it should prefer to defend.

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"action": "attack", "ability_name": null, "target": "exact target name"}}

JSON:

freeform_action_resolution.description: THE REFEREE for player-typed custom actions: judge feasibility, narrate, judge impact
freeform_action_resolution.prompt_template:
You are the referee of a monster battle. The player typed a CUSTOM action for their monster; you judge whether it is possible, then narrate what happens.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- Location ---
{location_name}

--- The acting monster (a member of the player's party) ---
{actor_details}

--- The player's custom action ---
Action text: {player_action_text}
Chosen target (if any): {player_target}
Additional information from the player: {player_info}

--- Current battle situation ---
{battle_situation}

--- Recent battle events ---
{recent_log}

--- The referee's mood for this expedition ---
{referee_hint}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
1. FIRST judge feasibility: the action is possible only if this specific monster could plausibly do it right now, given its body, abilities, personality, and condition. Wildly overpowered, absurd, or out-of-character actions are NOT possible. Reasonable creative uses of the monster's nature ARE possible.
2. If NOT possible: set possible to false, impact to "none", and write 1-2 sentences narrating the monster attempting it and failing or the moment passing - the turn is wasted.
3. If possible: set possible to true, write 1-2 sentences narrating the action playing out, and judge the impact ON THE AFFECTED MONSTER with one of:
   - "none": no direct effect on anyone's wellbeing
   - "light": a typical result - THE MOST COMMON
   - "heavy": strong, exploits a weakness
   - "devastating": rare, must feel earned
   - "heal_light" / "heal_major": restorative effects
4. Set impact_target to the exact name of the single monster whose wellbeing changes (or null if none does).
5. Judge the ACTOR's exertion: stamina_cost (physical effort) and mana_cost (magical effort), each exactly one of "none", "minor", "moderate", "heavy", "restore_minor", "restore_major". Honor any costs or restorative effects the action explicitly describes; otherwise assume sensibly (small physical feats are minor stamina, grand magical feats are moderate or heavy mana). An actor with 'spent' reserves in the needed pool should falter - prefer weak results and narrate the exhaustion.
6. If the actor carries an Affinity line: a DEVOTED monster attempting its partner's creative plan gets the benefit of the doubt on feasibility; a WARY one may hesitate in the narration.
7. Use numbers only as magnitude, never explicit math. The narration must match the impact and the exertion.

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"possible": true, "narration": "1-2 sentences.", "impact": "light", "impact_target": "exact monster name or null", "stamina_cost": "minor", "mana_cost": "none"}}

JSON:

battle_talk.description: Adjudicate battlefield negotiation and decide whether it changes the battle
battle_talk.prompt_template:
You speak for the hostile monsters in the middle of a battle, and you decide what comes of the words being exchanged.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- Location ---
{location_name}

--- The hostile monsters (you speak for these; their personalities, backstories, and wishes matter greatly) ---
{enemy_details}

--- The player's party (the other side of the exchange) ---
{ally_details}

--- Current battle situation ---
{battle_situation}

--- Recent battle events ---
{recent_log}

--- What was said ---
{exchange}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
1. Write the hostile monsters' spoken response, each strictly in its own Voice (its listed speech style), 2-4 sentences. Monsters that cannot speak answer through behavior.
2. JUDGE how the party's words land using each monster's own rubric where listed: words matching what it 'Responds well to' soften it; what it 'Responds poorly to' hardens it.
3. DECIDE what comes of this exchange, as ONE of:
   - "continue": the battle goes on (the most common outcome - words are cheap)
   - "enemies_join": the hostile monsters accept an offer to join the party. Only if the offer speaks to their wishes - or the very thing a monster 'Would join a party for' - AND their situation makes it appealing (losing, lonely, seeking what the party offers). Never while they are winning comfortably.
   - "enemies_yield": the hostile monsters give up and let the party pass, without joining
   - "enemies_flee": the hostile monsters break off and escape
   - "party_spared": the hostile monsters accept the party's plea for mercy and let them leave alive. Only plausible when the party is clearly losing and the monsters are not cruel.
4. Decisions must be EARNED by the battle state and the monsters' characters. When in doubt, choose "continue".

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"response": "The hostile monsters' spoken reply.", "decision": "continue"}}

JSON:

action_resolution.description: THE REFEREE: narrate one battle action, judge its impact, and judge the actor's exertion
action_resolution.prompt_template:
You are the referee of a monster battle. You narrate ONE action, judge its impact on the target, and judge what it costs the actor.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- Location ---
{location_name}

--- The acting monster ---
{actor_details}

--- The action being taken ---
{action_description}

--- The target of the action ---
{target_details}

--- Current battle situation ---
{battle_situation}

--- Recent battle events ---
{recent_log}

--- The referee's mood for this expedition ---
{referee_hint}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
1. Write 1-2 sentences narrating how this action plays out, true to both monsters' personalities, abilities, and SIDES (party vs hostile - check their tags above). Use numbers only to describe magnitude, never explicit math.
2. Judge the impact ON THE TARGET with exactly one of these words:
   - "none": the action misses, is shrugged off, or has no direct effect on the target's wellbeing (defending, posturing, buffs)
   - "light": a typical hit or glancing blow - THE MOST COMMON RESULT
   - "heavy": a strong hit that exploits a weakness or lands perfectly
   - "devastating": rare - a perfect, crushing blow that should feel earned
   - "heal_light": modest healing or relief for the target
   - "heal_major": powerful restoration for the target
3. Judge the ACTOR's exertion: what this action drains from its stamina (physical effort) and mana (magical effort). Use exactly one word for each, from: "none", "minor", "moderate", "heavy", "restore_minor", "restore_major".
   - HONOR anything the action or ability description explicitly states about costs: 'costs almost nothing' means none or minor; 'passively restores mana' or 'draws power back in' means restore_minor; 'exhausting' means heavy.
   - Otherwise assume sensibly: a basic attack is minor stamina; a modest physical ability is moderate stamina; a big spell is moderate or heavy mana; defending or resting restores a little stamina.
   - Check the actor's Reserves above. An actor with 'spent' reserves in the pool this action needs is running on fumes: its action should falter - prefer impact "none" or "light" and narrate the exhaustion or fizzled magic.
4. If the actor carries an Affinity line: a DEVOTED party monster gives everything for its partners - let its efforts land a shade more generously; a WARY one holds something back - it may hesitate mid-action (color the narration only, never invent a different action).
5. The narration must MATCH the impact and the exertion you choose.

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"narration": "1-2 sentences describing the action.", "impact": "light", "stamina_cost": "minor", "mana_cost": "none"}}

JSON:

turn_vanity.description: Streamed one-sentence present-moment thought for the party monster whose turn it is (the player decides the actual action)

battle_summary.description: Summarize a finished battle for the dungeon adventure log, including lasting effects
battle_summary.prompt_template:
You are the chronicler of a dungeon adventure, writing the record of a battle that just ended.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- Location ---
{location_name}

--- How the battle ended ---
Outcome: {outcome} for the player's party
How it ended: {resolution} (combat = fought to the end; joined = enemies joined the party; yielded = enemies gave up; fled = enemies escaped; spared = the party was shown mercy)
Monsters who joined the party: {joined_names}

--- The player's party (final conditions) ---
{party_details}

--- The hostile monsters (final conditions) ---
{enemy_details}

--- The full battle log, turn by turn ---
{recent_log}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Write a 3-4 sentence summary of this battle for the adventure log. Include:
1. Who fought and how the fight actually went (use the battle log - key blows, turning points, notable words spoken).
2. How it ended and what became of each hostile monster (fell, fled, yielded, joined).
3. Any LASTING effects that should be remembered going forward: injuries and conditions the party carries out, lingering ability effects or debuffs mentioned in the log (e.g. jammed gears, poison, curses), promises or threats made.
Write in plain past tense, factual but vivid. No headings, no lists - one flowing paragraph.

Response:

battle_victory.description: Celebratory narration after the party defeats all enemies
battle_victory.prompt_template:
You are the narrator of a fantasy dungeon adventure. The player's party has just WON a battle.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- Location ---
{location_name}

--- How it ended ---
{resolution} ('combat' = the enemies fell in battle; 'joined' = they agreed to join the party; 'yielded' = they gave up and let the party pass; 'fled' = they escaped)

--- The victorious party ---
{party_details}

--- The other monsters ---
{enemy_details}

--- The party's adventure so far ---
{dungeon_log}

--- The final moments of the battle ---
{recent_log}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Write 2-3 sentences describing this ending and its aftermath, true to how it ended. If monsters joined, celebrate the new companions. Reflect the party members' personalities.

Response:

battle_defeat.description: Somber narration after all party monsters are defeated
battle_defeat.prompt_template:
You are the narrator of a fantasy dungeon adventure. The player's party has just LOST a battle.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- Location ---
{location_name}

--- How it ended ---
{resolution} ('combat' = the party was overwhelmed and retreats from the dungeon with their fallen companions; 'spared' = the hostile monsters accepted the party's plea and let them leave alive)

--- The defeated party ---
{party_details}

--- The victorious monsters ---
{enemy_details}

--- The party's adventure so far ---
{dungeon_log}

--- The final moments of the battle ---
{recent_log}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Write 2-3 sentences of somber text describing this ending. It is a setback, not the end; the party escapes with their lives - humbled, but alive.

Response:
